title: Balania formosana
intro: Balania formosana, also known as Formosan balania, is a plant species that belongs to the family Santalaceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Balania formosana include terpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared in the form of infusions, decoctions, and extracts, and is commonly used to make teas, tinctures, and capsules.
taxonomy: Balania formosana is a species of flowering plant in the family Salicaceae. It is native to Taiwan and is also known as Taiwan balania or Formosan balania. This evergreen tree is classified as a member of the genus Balania. The exact classification of Balania formosana may vary depending on the taxonomic source. Balania formosana is considered a unique and endemic species to the island of Taiwan.
